title: Sanalabs HR Agent Library — Accessibility & UX Redesign
description:
# Sanalabs was hiding its most powerful HR workflows behind a wall of accessibility failures.

## The Problem
The Workflows screen in Sanalabs ships a two-column library of HR automation prompts that looks clean at first glance but breaks apart the moment you stress-test it. Every card carries an identical grey lightning-bolt icon with no distinguishing label and no programmatic alternative, a direct violation of **WCAG 2.2 — 1.1.1 Non-text Content (A)**, which requires all non-text content to carry a meaningful text alternative. Alongside this, the secondary metadata text rendering 'Used 0 times · HR' sits well below the 4.5:1 contrast threshold mandated by **WCAG 2.2 — 1.4.3 Contrast Minimum (AA)**, making it functionally invisible for users with low vision or anyone working in bright ambient light. Compound these issues with the complete absence of visible keyboard focus indicators — breaking **WCAG 2.2 — 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA)** — and you have a screen that keyboard-only users and switch-access users simply cannot operate.

## Impact on Users
A screen reader user lands on this page and hears a sequence of indistinguishable icons followed by truncated descriptions that cut off before communicating any real intent, leaving them with no reliable way to evaluate or select a workflow. Sighted users fare only marginally better: descriptions are clipped mid-sentence with no expand affordance, which **Universal Principles of Design — Progressive Disclosure** identifies as a failure to give users what they need at each decision point, forcing unnecessary navigation just to preview content.

## What We Changed
- Assigned unique ARIA labels to each workflow icon and marked decorative instances with aria-hidden='true', resolving the Non-text Content failure
- Darkened all metadata text to #595959 on white, achieving a comfortable contrast ratio well above the 4.5:1 AA minimum
- Applied a 3px solid focus outline via CSS :focus-visible across every interactive element, restoring full keyboard navigability
- Added a hover and focus tooltip revealing the full workflow prompt without requiring navigation away from the list, honouring Progressive Disclosure
- Relabelled the paired filter dropdowns as 'Category' and 'Agent Type' with visible group labels, reducing decision friction per **Psychology of Design — Hick's Law**
- Suppressed the 'Used 0 times' counter and replaced it with a 'New' badge, eliminating the negative social proof signal that **Designing User Interfaces (Malewicz)** warns actively erodes user trust

## The Result
The redesigned Workflows screen is fully keyboard navigable, meets WCAG 2.2 AA across all audited criteria, and presents workflow content in a way that helps users make faster, more confident decisions without leaving the library view.
tags: sanalabs, ux design, accessibility, wcag, ui design, product design, saas design, ai tools, interaction design, usability
